skill.name: agent-framework-azure-ai-py
skill.description: Build Azure AI Foundry agents with the Microsoft Agent Framework Python SDK. The skill covers persistent agents, hosted tools, MCP integration, conversation threads, streaming responses, function tools, and structured outputs.
skill.summary: Create Azure AI Foundry agents with Python, hosted tools, MCP, threads, streaming, and structured outputs.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Most static findings are false positives caused by Markdown fences, Python imports, and Python async context managers in documentation examples. The skill is still medium risk because it instructs users to configure hosted code execution, web search, and MCP network tools for Azure AI agents.

medium_findings.title: Hosted Code, Web Search, and MCP Tool Enablement
medium_findings.description: The skill demonstrates hosted code execution, Bing web search, and MCP network tooling inside Azure AI agents. This is legitimate agent development guidance, but deployments should restrict tool permissions, review outbound endpoints, and avoid sending sensitive data to hosted tools.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced examples explicitly configure hosted code, web search, and MCP tools. The intent appears educational rather than malicious, so the concern is operational risk rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Example Service URLs
low_findings.description: The skill includes an Azure project endpoint placeholder and a Microsoft Learn MCP endpoint in examples. These are not evidence of credential exfiltration, but users should verify endpoints before production use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible in documentation examples and point to Azure or Microsoft services. No evidence found that they collect secrets or route traffic to an unrelated destination.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Pattern Matches Are Mostly Documentation False Positives
dangerous_patterns.description: The reported dynamic import, with-statement, shell-backtick, and weak-cryptography hits occur in Markdown examples or ordinary text. No evidence found that SKILL.md executes code, performs hidden command execution, or uses weak cryptography.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Line review confirms these hits are Markdown fences, Python async context managers, Python import statements, table text, or frontmatter text. They are not executable malware indicators in this skill file.

content.user_title: Build Azure AI Foundry Python Agents
content.value_statement: Developers need reliable patterns for persistent Azure AI agents with tools and conversation state. This skill provides Python examples for agents, hosted tools, MCP, threads, streaming, and structured responses.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to create Azure AI Foundry agents with AzureAIAgentsProvider.
Demonstrates Python function tools with typed parameters and Pydantic descriptions.
Explains hosted tool setup for code interpreter, file search, web search, and MCP.
Covers conversation threads for persistent multi-turn agent workflows.
Includes streaming response examples and structured output validation with Pydantic.
Provides a complete multi-tool research assistant example.
limitations: Requires Azure AI Foundry resources and valid Azure credentials.
Uses pre-release package installation examples, so APIs may change.
Does not include deployment automation, monitoring, or production security policy.
Hosted tools and MCP endpoints may create extra cost or data exposure.

use_cases.title: Prototype an Azure AI Agent
use_cases.description: Create a basic persistent agent with Azure credentials, model deployment settings, and a simple run call.

use_cases.title: Add Tools to an Agent Workflow
use_cases.description: Combine function tools, hosted web search, code interpreter, and MCP access in one Azure AI agent.

use_cases.title: Design Stateful Agent Conversations
use_cases.description: Use threads, streaming responses, and structured outputs to support repeatable multi-turn assistant workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Agent
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for Azure AI Foundry
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a minimal Python Azure AI Foundry agent. Include required environment variables, authentication, provider setup, and one simple agent run.

prompt_templates.title: Add Function Tools
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add two Python function tools to an Azure AI agent. Include type annotations, Pydantic Field descriptions, and a sample user request.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Stateful Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design an Azure AI agent workflow with conversation threads and streaming output. Explain how the thread is created and reused.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Multi-Tool Agent
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review a planned Azure AI agent that uses code interpreter, web search, and MCP. Identify permission boundaries, data risks, and safer configuration choices.

output_examples.input: Create a starter Azure AI Foundry agent in Python.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan with environment variables, Azure credential selection, provider setup, agent creation, and a first test prompt.

output_examples.input: Add web search and MCP to my research assistant agent.
output: Recommended hosted tool choices for search and MCP access.
Notes about endpoint review, data handling, and tool permission limits.
A workflow outline for non-streaming and streaming agent responses.

output_examples.input: Explain how to keep context across agent turns.
output_examples.output: Guidance for creating an agent thread, passing it into each run call, and storing the conversation identifier for later resumption.

best_practices: Use least-privilege Azure credentials and separate development from production identity.
Limit hosted tools to the specific agent task and review each external endpoint.
Validate structured outputs with strict Pydantic models before downstream use.
anti_patterns: Do not pass secrets, private files, or regulated data into web search or MCP tools without approval.
Do not enable code interpreter for agents that only need text generation.
Do not rely on pre-release APIs without version pinning and regression tests.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps you build Azure AI Foundry agents with the Microsoft Agent Framework Python SDK.

faq.question: Does it require Azure credentials?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples use Azure CLI credentials for development and DefaultAzureCredential for production.

faq.question: Can it support multi-turn conversations?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill explains agent threads and conversation identifiers for persistent context.

faq.question: Does it cover tool calling?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers Python function tools, hosted tools, web search, code interpreter, file search, and MCP.

faq.question: Is this safe for production use?
faq.answer: Use it as implementation guidance. Production systems need endpoint review, permission limits, logging, and data handling controls.

faq.question: Which assistants can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
